lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You need to try and find the source of the Sleeper Plague. I'd start in the church. That's where they're looking after the victims.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a plan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Veliaf? What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met friend. I'm here on an investigation. There's something strange going on in this town, and I suspect the vampyres are involved.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything I can help with?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Not at the moment. Keep checking in though, I may have need of you soon.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well an extra pair of hands is always beneficial. If you have the time of course?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'm a bit busy right now. Maybe later.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Very well, friend. Myreque prevail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. What do you need?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: This town has recently become home to an illness known as the Sleeper Plague. It's a nasty affliction. Those infected fall into a deep sleep, never to wake.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Now, Morytania is no stranger to odd afflictions. However, I've been doing some digging. Apparently, this isn't the first time this illness has beset Slepe. In fact, they say it's how the town was named.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And now the illness is back. You said you suspect the vampyres are involved?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I do. The first victim of the new Sleeper Plague was none other than Lady Crombwick. She rules over this town from Crombwick Manor, along with her husband.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: He's not been seen since she fell ill, and their manor has been locked down.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Here's the interesting part though. Vampyres have been seen around the manor. Apparently they showed up around the time when the Sleeper Plague returned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see where this is going. You think the vampyres are the reason the plague is back.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I do. The question is, why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess that's for us to work out. Where do we start?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: We'll split up. I'm going to try and learn more about these vampyres at the manor. While I do that, I want you to try and find the source of the illness.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: They're looking after the victims in this church here. I'd recommend you make that your starting point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a plan. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: And to you. Myreque prevail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How have things been for you recently?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: We haven't been doing too bad. Even out in Mort Myre, the impact of Ranis' death can be seen.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: People have a little bit more hope now, and it has become easier for us to gather support.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's good to know.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Of course, there are downsides. The vampyres are hunting us with ever greater determination. But this is the life we chose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about this place?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Slepe? It's wretched. The people of this town follow the teachings of Zamorak. Some are no better than the vampyres themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do they get on with the vampyres?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: As well as any humans can. All settlements in Morytania must pay the vampyres in blood tithes, but the people of Slepe do so willingly.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: They see the vampyres as fellow servants of Zamorak. Of course, the vampyres only really serve themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why do these people follow Zamorak?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Apparently this place was founded by a group called the Sisterhood, former healers of Saradomin that turned to Zamorak.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Their order still exists to this day. You'll probably see a few of them around the town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me any more about the Sleeper Plague?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: What I told you is pretty much what I know. The illness causes people to fall to sleep, never to wake again.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: There's a rumour that the infected are also granted immortality, though I don't know if there's any truth in this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be honest, immortality doesn't seem very useful if you're unable to wake up.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Indeed. If history is to be believed, this is the second time the Sleeper Plague has hit Slepe. The first occurrence was many years ago and is supposedly what gave the town its name.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: That's all I know. That said, I suspect you might be able to learn more if you do some digging around town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'm a bit busy right now. Maybe later.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Very well friend. Myreque prevail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't look too happy?

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: You're not wrong about that. It's my travelling companion, Hanchen. They say he's the latest victim of the Sleeper Plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know how he fell ill?

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: Like I said, I've no idea. We'd just been to the pub when it happened. Hanchen's always loved a good drink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met friend. How goes the investigation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently the latest victim of the Sleeper Plague was at the pub shortly before falling ill.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Interesting. Might be worth a look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Agreed. I'll see if I can discover anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't look too happy?

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: You're not wrong about that. It's my travelling companion, Hanchen. They say he's the latest victim of the Sleeper Plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know how he fell ill?

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: I've no idea. We'd just been to the pub for a quick drink. On our way back, he started to act all odd, then he just collapsed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pub?

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: Yes. The Rat & Bat just south east of here. I'm not a drinker myself but Hanchen's always loved a good tipple.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... you mean to say you weren't drinking?

lines.speaker: Hameln the Jester
lines.text: No, I never drink. Hanchen on the other hand was loving those Bloody Bracers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What ales are you serving?

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: We serve Crombwick's finest Bloody Bracer, that is all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds rough, how much?

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: 99 of your finest coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do you get your Bloody Bracers from?

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: You don't give up do you. I'm not telling you anything so clear off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I might just have a look around anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What ales are you serving?

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: We serve Crombwick's finest Bloody Bracer, that is all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds rough, how much?

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: 99 of your finest coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do you get your Bloody Bracers from?

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: It's an old recipe of the Crombwick family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. But the actual Bloody Bracers you sell, where do they come from?

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: Who's asking?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... me of course.

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: Yes, but what I mean is, why do you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does it matter?

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: My supplier is a private individual. He doesn't like others meddling in his affairs. So yes, it does matter.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I represent... health?

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: Health? What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... Health and safety of course, here on a routine inspection.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Part of my work requires me to inspect the source of your Bloody Bracers. I was hoping you'd put me in touch with your supplier.

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: I don't know what you're on about. There're no routine inspections here. Now clear off, I have customers to serve!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I might just have a look around anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. Looks like Carl is pretty much out of Bloody Bracer. He'll need to collect more from his supplier soon.

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: Huh. Looks like we're all out of Bloody Bracer again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright then Carl. Let's see what you're hiding...

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: Hey! I see you there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The dungeon Carl? What are you hiding down there?

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: Carl? What do you want?

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: Kroy. We've run out of Bloody Bracer again.

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: Did you really need to come here to tell me that?

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: There's something else as well. Someone's been poking around, asking about Bloody Bracer.

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: I don't know. They don't look to be from around here.

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: So someone's been snooping around asking questions, and you decided to come here? What if they followed you?

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: I... I thought you'd want to know.

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: You're a fool Carl. If you're not careful, you'll jeopardise everything.

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: Hey! I don't need to help you!

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: No, you don't. Your predecessor didn't want to help us. Perhaps you remember what came of him?

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: Go back to your pub Carl. I'll have more Bloody Bracer sent over soon. In the meantime, try to be a bit smarter.

lines.speaker: Carl
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Alright then, Damien. What is it that you're so keen to show me?

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: Vanstrom, I'd like you to meet Kroy.

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: Greetings, my lord.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Don't tell me you had me come all the way down here for this? Look at the wretched thing, Damien. It's barely even a proper juvenile.

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: Well just hang on there, Vanstrom. Kroy may not look like much, but I've never met a vampyre with a better knowledge of herblore.

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: Kroy, tell him what you found.

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: Of course, my lord.

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: It was hidden deep within the sanctuary here. A recipe, for an elixir.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: An elixir?

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: Yes! The ones that built the place, the Sisterhood, they called it the Elixir of Everlasting.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: And just what is this Elixir of Everlasting?

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: Those who drink it find their lives heavily extended, but at a cost. They are put into a deep sleep, never to wake.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: So the Sleeper Plague? That was your doing?

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: Indeed. Using the recipe, Kroy was easily able to recreate the elixir. After a quick test on Lady Crombwick, we started giving it to the citizens.

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: We hide it in their beer, Bloody Bracer. They've no idea what it's really doing to them!

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: And while they sleep, we tithe them. The sleepers only need a fraction of the blood a normal human needs, so we can take more than ever before!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: And tell me, Damien, who gave you permission to do this?

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: The nobles of Darkmeyer make their demands Vanstrom. Someone has to meet them.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: That someone is not you. I am in charge of blood tithing. I alone decide how it is to be carried out.

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: The nobles are restless. They are tired of inaction. I saw an opportunity and I took it. Lord Drakan should thank me.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I don't doubt the ingenuity of your plan. This elixir could have great potential. The thing I doubt is your recklessness. This Sleeper Plague has attracted attention. The wrong kind of attention.

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: The Myreque? Don't tell me you're scared of that lot, Vanstrom? I thought you were made of stronger stuff.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I do not fear the Myreque. I have no need to. They have no way of touching me.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: But you on the other hand, you should be very afraid. Out here alone, without the support of the Drakan household. You're a prime target. And if what I hear is true, they're right on your doorstep.

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: What's that supposed to mean?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: The Myreque are already here in Slepe, Damien. The one that killed Ranis Drakan is among them. Your overconfidence has begun your downfall.

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: Well you'll have to do something. You'll need to send us some support.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I will do no such thing. You defied Lord Drakan when you came out here. The consequences of your actions are your own to deal with.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Now, if you'll excuse me, I will be on my way. There are things in Darkmeyer that require my attention.

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: How much did you hear, human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Enough.

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: I see. Then you know that I can't let you leave this place alive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tempting offer. Death isn't on my to-do list today though, so I'll have to decline.

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: A joker? Your arrogance will be your end, human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll see.

lines.speaker: Kroy
lines.text: Yes... We shall...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's him dealt with. I should destroy his lab as well. Don't want anyone else continuing his work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There. That should stop anyone from continuing Kroy's work. I should inform Veliaf.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met friend. Anything to report?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You were right about the vampyres. I did some digging and discovered that they are indeed the cause of the Sleeper Plague.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A vampyre in the dungeon called Kroy was making infected Bloody Bracers. The local barkeep was then selling them to the townspeople.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I put a stop to that though. I killed Kroy and destroyed his lab. The barkeep has no supply now.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Excellent work friend. This should stop any new cases of the illness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's something else, too. While I was spying on Kroy, I saw Vanstrom Klause with another vampyre called Damien. It sounded like Damien was Kroy's boss.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: The vampyre you speak of is a noble called Damien Leucurte. He's a known quantity to us. My investigations at the manor also involved the two of them, but let's hear the rest of your findings first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, in their conversations, it was revealed why they caused the new Sleeper Plague. Apparently, they did it to make it easier to take blood tithes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But here's the interesting thing. Vanstrom didn't seem very happy with this. It sounded like Damien was doing it without his permission.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: This is all very interesting. The citizens of this town are Zamorakians and willingly give their blood to the vampyres. Why would Damien need the Sleeper Plague?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He talked about increased demand from the nobles of Darkmeyer. Perhaps they have need for more blood than normal?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: That would make sense. I've heard rumours that the vampyre population has grown such that demand for blood now exceeds supply. Damien may be looking for ways to satisfy this demand.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Even so, this seems extreme. And to go behind Vanstrom's back as well? Maybe their overcrowding issues are bigger than we first realised.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe. So, what's our next move?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I have an idea about that, although my information is not quite as exciting as yours I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: While I was investigating the manor, I discovered that it has indeed been taken over by vampyres. Our friend Damien seems to be the leader of this group.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: During my time there, I also managed to spy on Vanstrom and Damien. I only heard a small part of their conversation, but Vanstrom didn't seem very happy, which matches up with what you heard.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: At one point, Vanstrom insulted Damien for his recklessness and accused him of demanding too much from the werewolves. Sounds like they've also been gathering extra blood without permission.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds familiar. I suspect it was werewolves who attacked me that time at Paterdomus.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Yes, that encounter also sprung into my mind. It sounds like things are even worse now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you want to investigate the werewolves?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I do indeed. Something is going on in Darkmeyer that's causing this increased demand. If there's unrest in the city, we might be able to use it to our advantage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about Slepe? Should we not deal with Damien as well?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Not yet. Your actions will have drawn attention to us. We cannot stay here. It sounds like Damien can do little without Kroy, so I doubt he'll be much trouble anyway.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Besides, we've already done too much for these Zamorakians. There are others more deserving of our time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But aren't we meant to help people?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: That's a complicated question friend. But yes, of course we are meant to help people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then why are we letting Damien get away with this? Carl as well?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Damien and Carl are small fish in a very big pond. Dealing with them might help the people here, but it will mean nothing to the rest of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: If we want to help as many people as possible, we need to think bigger than that. The Drakan family, they are the real targets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose. It just seems harsh to leave these people in the hands of a vampyre like Damien.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: We've already done a lot to help here. Besides, Damien will get what's coming to him in the end, I'm sure of that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're sure this isn't just because they're Zamorakian?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Zamorakians are evil my friend, it does not shame me to say it. But no, if this were a Saradominist town, my actions would be the same.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, fair enough.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You have a good heart my friend. I have a lot of respect for that. I assure you that if we keep on our current path, we will bring freedom to all of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, Veliaf. So, what's our next move?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Meet me at Paterdomus friend. We will check in with Drezel and Ivan, before continuing our investigation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll meet you there.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Be careful out there. Myreque prevail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough, so what now?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Meet me at Paterdomus friend. We will check in with Drezel and Ivan, before continuing our investigation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll meet you there.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Be careful out there. Myreque prevail.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Meet me in the Haunted Woods, just south of Fenkenstrain's Castle. We will hopefully find Ivan there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see you there.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: You should head for the Haunted Woods, friend. With luck, you'll find Ivan just south of Fenkenstrain's Castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about the Seven Priestly Warriors?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Well at this point you should be no stranger to their tale, though I understand the desire for a recap.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: During the Fourth Age, the vampyre forces of Lowerniel Drakan attempted to invade Misthalin. They would have succeeded, were it not for the Seven Priestly Warriors.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: The warriors were the ones to bless the River Salve and build this temple, ensuring that Misthalin would be forever safe from the evils of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Sadly, all seven of them fell in the final battle against Drakan. They are now buried here, in this very mausoleum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know if that's completely true.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: You talk of the suggestion that Ivandis Seergaze isn't actually buried with the others, don't you? That isn't a conversation I wish to have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. Anything else you can tell me about the Warriors?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Not really. You might learn more in the library below, but I'd be cautious of believing every word in those books.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. Thanks Drezel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm on it.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met friend. Good to see you made it in one piece.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Indeed, I hear Morytania becomes more dangerous by the day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can say that again. Good to see you Drezel. Has Veliaf already filled you in?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Not yet, I thought it best to wait for you.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: But you're here now, so let's get started.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well Drezel, the reason we are here is due to an investigation we've been conducting in Slepe.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Ah. I hear it's a nasty place, full of Zamorakian filth. Suffering from some sort of outbreak as well, I've been told.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, the Sleeper Plague. It turns out, however, that the vampyres are responsible.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Seems that one of their nobles, Damien Leucurte, has been looking for ways to increase blood output. Interestingly, he's been doing it without consent from the Drakan family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. Vanstrom doesn't seem to be very happy about it.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Interesting. It's odd you should come to report this, because we've also experienced some strange happenings around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm. Do these strange happenings have anything to do with werewolves, by any chance?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: They do.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I knew it. What's been going on?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Well, after [player name] here was ambushed outside the temple a while back, Ivan and I decided to keep a closer eye on things.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: For a time, nothing unusual happened. The reports of trouble in the area died down, and everything seemed normal.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Perhaps their encounter with you scared them off, [player name].

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Maybe. But if so, it was only for a time. A few weeks ago, the reports of people going missing in the area started again. And they're much worse this time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Entire groups have been going missing. Not just miners and travellers either. Even some of the king's mercenaries have vanished.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Did you send word to the king?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: We did. His adviser though, Aeonisig, encouraged him to refrain from military action.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Typical. This is why I hate politics.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Has anyone seen who's been doing this? Is it definitely werewolves?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: We believe so. Ivan has been keeping a close watch. The disappearances have mostly taken place further up the pass, however, so it's taken a while for him to find any leads.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: But he has found one?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Yes, last night. He overheard a hooded figure mention a meeting in the Haunted Woods, directly south of Fenkenstrain's Castle, that is apparently set for today.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perfect. Sounds like that's where we should head next then.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: On the topic of Ivan, where is he Drezel?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Ah... Well that's just the thing. He went to spy on the meeting.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: What? You just let him go? He's just a boy!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: He's a grown man, Veliaf! One far smarter than either you or I. If he wants to do something, he will do it. I can't stop him!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You should have sent word to us! This is for us to deal with, not him!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: There wasn't time. He only found out about the meeting last night!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ivan is still a member of the Myreque, isn't he? Surely he can look after himself.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: That's not the point! He needs to be kept safe!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand. Why is Ivan so important?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Ivan... Well, Ivan is special. He is the last in his bloodline.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what bloodline is that?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Ivan is the last living descendant of Ivandis Seergaze himself. Once I am gone, he will be the only remaining descendant of the Seven Priestly Warriors.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Are you becoming forgetful, [player name]? The Seven Priestly Warriors were the ones who originally blessed the River Salve.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Without their sacrifice, Drakan and his kin would have long since taken over Misthalin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes, I remember. So, this is why you want to keep Ivan safe, Veliaf?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. So that's why you want to keep him safe, Veliaf?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: It's not just about that. There's also the promise I made. Before his mother died, I swore to her on my life that I would shield Ivan from any harm.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Though I nearly failed her when Vanstrom killed Sani and Harold.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Veliaf, my friend, you and I will not be around to protect Ivan forever. We have guided him to this point, but maybe it's time for him to start looking after himself.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: And what of his learnings here? You would have him abandon them?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I have taught Ivan everything I know. There is nothing more he can gain from me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Forgive me, but this can be resolved later. For now, we need to find Ivan and see what this meeting is about.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Yes, you're absolutely right, friend. Forgive us. Directly south of Fenkenstrain's Castle. Is that right Drezel?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: That's right. In the Haunted Woods.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Very well. Meet me there, [player name]. Let's see if we can find out what's going on.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Oh, and [player name], I would appreciate it if you could avoid mentioning the stuff about Ivandis Seergaze to Ivan. One day he will have to bear that burden. But for now, he has enough weight on his shoulders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I understand.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Good luck to you both. Saradomin be with you.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: [player name]! So good to see you. What brings you out here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, Ivan. Believe it or not, I'm actually here for you.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: For me? I'm guessing Drezel sent you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. He told us of your discovery about the werewolves. We came to lend a hand.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: We? Is Veliaf with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. We've been investigating something together. We suspect it may be connected to this situation with the werewolves. I imagine he'll be here shortly.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Veliaf!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Ivan. It's good to see you safe.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: And you. [player name] says you're here about the werewolves.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Indeed. But Ivan, you shouldn't be out here. It's not safe for you. Head back to the temple, let [player name] and I take it from here.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Don't worry, Veliaf. I can look after myself.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Ivan...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! Look!

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: You're late.

lines.speaker: Werewolf
lines.text: Well look who it is. Damien Leucurte! I thought you didn't like to associate with our lot.

lines.speaker: Werewolf
lines.text: Scared of getting mud on his pretty clothes, I bet.

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: Mind your tongue, dog, or I'll have you put in a collar.

lines.speaker: Werewolf
lines.text: Your threats mean nothing out here Damien. There're no Drakans around to protect you.

lines.speaker: Werewolf
lines.text: Not that Drakan protection means much these days anyway. His own brother killed by a human. How does he respond? He stays hiding in his fancy castle.

lines.speaker: Werewolf
lines.text: Ha! You think this one would get Drakan protection even if he were about? I hear Vanstrom has found out about this little side venture. Little Damien has been a very naughty boy.

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: Enough of this! What do you have for me?

lines.speaker: Werewolf
lines.text: A nice family of three, out for a little picnic a bit too close to the Salve.

lines.speaker: Prisoner
lines.text: Please... don't hurt us. We'll do anything!

lines.speaker: Werewolf
lines.text: Shut up!

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: Three? Is that all?

lines.speaker: Werewolf
lines.text: Well if you want more, you're welcome to cross the Salve yourself and get them.

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: There's been a setback with our operations in Slepe. We need you to make up the shortfall. Next time, bring more.

lines.speaker: Werewolf
lines.text: You assume there's going to be a next time. I bet you don't last another week now that the Myreque are running around with vampyre-killing weapons.

lines.speaker: Werewolf
lines.text: A week? He won't even last another day at the rate he's going!

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: I tire of your arrogance. We're done here.

lines.speaker: Werewolf
lines.text: Yes, we are. See you around Damien.

lines.speaker: Werewolf
lines.text: Or not.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: So it's as we suspected. The werewolves have been taking people from across the Salve.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But they've been doing so without permission, just like in Slepe.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: This doesn't give us anything more to go on though. We have our suspicions, but we still don't know for sure why they are doing this.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Should we follow them? Maybe we'll learn more if we do. We might also get a chance to save that poor family.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: No, it's too risky. Last thing we need is you getting captured, Ivan.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Hang on... Do you two hear something?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Go on then! Finish it!

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Oh Veliaf my dear, If I'd wanted you dead I'd have done it a long time ago.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: You're not here to kill us?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: No... I'm here to talk...

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: So... Let's talk.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I take it you know who I am?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: You're Vanescula Drakan, sister to Lowerniel Drakan.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: And formally, sister to Ranis Drakan, before [player name] here came along.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Ah yes, [player name], the great vampyre slayer. I would thank you, were it not for the fact that his funeral was such a dull affair.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... You wanted him dead?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: There was no love between Ranis and I. You did us a favour when you got rid of him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a shame. Maybe I shouldn't have bothered.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Oh on the contrary, the death of Ranis was just what we needed.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Save your words. You didn't come to thank us for Ranis. Why are you here?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Ah, and there I was thinking we were starting to become friends. Fine. I'm here to make you an offer. A once in a lifetime offer.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Which is?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: You've already killed one of my brothers. Now I offer you the chance to kill the other.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Alone, you have no hope of ever getting close to Lowerniel. Now, I on the otherhand, can get you right into his chambers at Castle Drakan.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: And why would you want that?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I have my reasons.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Care to share them?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I will, but not here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where then?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: To the south east of Meiyerditch, there's an old graveyard. The Icyene Graveyard, we call it. Gather the Myreque, every member, and meet me there. That is where I will present my proposal.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Every member of the Myreque? Do you take us for fools? You're trying to lure us all there to wipe us out.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: As I said to you earlier, Veliaf, if I wanted you dead I'd have done it long ago.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You're bluffing!

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Am I? So if I were to head to the Burgh de Rott inn and go down into the basement, what would I find?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You...

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: If I were to give the order, I could have the heads of every member of the Myreque mounted above the gates of Castle Drakan before the day is out.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Don't be so sure. There's more of us than you think.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Is there now? Do you refer to the elusive Calsidiu, the apparent leader of the Myreque?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: A lie, I'm afraid. I know exactly how many of you there are. And that number isn't as big as you'd like to pretend.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: A lie? Veliaf...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've made your point Vanescula. How do we get to this graveyard?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: A boat will be your easiest way I imagine. I'll let you worry about the details.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Incidentally, should you fail to attend...

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You'll mount our heads above the gates of Castle Drakan. Yeah, we get it.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Excellent, glad we understand one another. I will await you at the graveyard.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: This isn't good.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Veliaf... You lied about Calsidiu and the other Myreque?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Yes. But it was with good intention, Ivan. To believe there were more of us out there... It inspired hope in our ranks, and fear in our enemies.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: A false hope.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Better than none at all. Ivan, I have made some tough choices over my years, but every one of them was for the good of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Okay... I understand. I don't like it, but I understand.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I don't like it either. Later, we will sit down and properly discuss this. However, at the moment I fear we have more pressing matters.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Vanescula. What do we do?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Go back to Paterdomus, Ivan. [player name] and I will handle it.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: But you heard what she said. She asked for all the Myreque. Veliaf, I'm one of the Myreque, I have to come with you.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Come with us? What makes you think we're even going? What makes you think we have any intention of listening to that witch?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: We don't have a choice, Veliaf, you know that. If we don't go, she'll kill us all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I fear Ivan is right. It doesn't seem like Vanescula has given us any other options.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Exactly. The decision has already been made for us, so I see little point in debating it. We're going. All of us.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Dammit Ivan! I'm trying to keep you safe!

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: You can't keep me safe, Veliaf. No one can. While Lowerniel Drakan lives, none of us will ever be safe.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: We have to do this. It's the only choice we have.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Fine. But don't expect me to even pretend to be happy about this! Breaking bread with a vampyre! What have we come to?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: We're not breaking bread with her. We're going to see what she has to offer. If she tries anything, [player name] here can deal with her, just like he did to her brother.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It would be my pleasure. What's our plan?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I will travel down to Burgh de Rott and inform Radigad and Polmafi of this plight we find ourselves in. I'll also have word sent to Safalaan and the others in Meiyerditch.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: [player name], I would appreciate it if you could escort Ivan down to Burgh de Rott.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: I need to collect some things from Paterdomus first. I'll update Drezel while I'm there. Once I'm done, I'll meet you outside the temple, on the Canifis side.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good to me.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I will see you both at the boathouse in Burgh de Rott. We can use the boat there to get to this graveyard. Good luck.

sections.title: Talking to Ivan during Temple Trekking

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Hey [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: I'm good. The swamps haven't beaten me yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are things between you and Veliaf?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Oh don't be worrying about that. Veliaf and I butt heads all the time. It's harmless, though. He's like a father to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He seems to worry a lot about you.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: He thinks I'd be safer back with Drezel. I can't stay hidden away forever though. Drezel's teachings were valuable, but I can ultimately do more good out here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what of the Calsidiu stuff?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Hmm. I still need to think on that. Ask me again later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of this Vanescula business?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: It's dangerous, but we don't have any choice. Besides, we're no strangers to danger.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Who knows, maybe this will even work out for us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And if it doesn't?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Keep that flail of yours nearby.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's get going.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Well that's torn it... Looks like we can't get over this bog. We'll have to go back to Paterdomus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm sure we can find a way through!

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: That's amazing! I'll have to do the same.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: That was great! But we really should be heading off now.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Rotten luck! The bridge is out. Do you think there's a way over?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I reckon we can come up with something.

lines.speaker: Vampyre Juvinate
lines.text: And what do we have here? Looks like some fresh Myreque meat!

lines.speaker: Vampyre Juvinate
lines.text: Hang on... That's the Slayer! The one that killed Ranis Drakan!

lines.speaker: Vampyre Juvinate
lines.text: Uh oh...

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Another successful journey. Thanks, [player name].

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: I'll meet you down at the boathouse. I imagine Veliaf is there already.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Hey there [player name]. I think I'm all ready to depart when you are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's our plan?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: We need to travel through Mort Myre Swamp to Burgh de Rott. We may encounter all kinds of evil on our way, so you'd probably best prepare to fight.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Are you ready to go to Burgh de Rott?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Then let us depart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to prepare first.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Okay, I'll be here once you're ready.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf at Burgh de Rott

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Hop in the boat once you're ready, friend. We're right behind you.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Hey. You should get into the boat if you're ready to go.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well, here we are. Likely going to our doom.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: At least we go together.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Touching, but ultimately meaningless.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Ever the optimist, aren't you Veliaf?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is everything ready?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Radigad has scouted out the graveyard. It's empty, for now. Vanescula's probably waiting for all of us to arrive before she makes her move.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What of Safalaan and the others?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Polmafi managed to get in contact with them. They will meet us there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess we'd better get going then.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Indeed. No point delaying the inevitable. Hop in the boat once you're ready, friend. We're right behind you.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Good to see you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you, Kael.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: You'd best talk to Veliaf and Safalaan. They are the big bosses after all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go speak to them.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Well met friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Radigad. Good to see you.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: I hope you came well armed my friend. These are dangerous times.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When are they not?

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: True. It looks like Veliaf and Safalaan are waiting for you. We will talk more later.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Hey [player name]. Looks like Veliaf and Safalaan want to speak to you. Let's talk after.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. See you soon.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Ah, [player name]. Glad to have you with us. Myreque prevail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good to see you Vertida. How are things?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: To be honest, I don't know. Ask me again if we're still alive after this meeting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. Talk later.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Hello there. Have you spoken to Veliaf and Safalaan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Ah, probably best that you do. That vampyre could arrive at any moment.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Veliaf! It has been far too long my friend.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Good to see you, Safalaan. Though I wish it were under better circumstances.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Indeed. And [player name], good to see you as well. Myreque prevail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Myreque prevail, Safalaan. How are things in Meiyerditch?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: As dark as ever, my friend. The vyrewatch have doubled their efforts to find us since the death of Ranis and the citizens are being tithed more than ever before.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: That said, it does feel like there is now a glimmer of hope where previously there was only darkness. While more ferocious, the vampyres also seem a bit more cautious now.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: They know we can kill them. They finally know what fear feels like.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: We're also seeing greater support from the people than ever before. Whereas once they'd only speak of the Myreque in whispers, now they speak of us loudly and with pride.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Anyway, let's focus on the matter at hand. I must say Veliaf, over the years I've come to expect the unexpected, but your message was something else altogether.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I mean, Vanescula Drakan herself! We find ourselves in very strange times.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Strange, and also dangerous. I do not like any aspect of this.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Well I won't pretend this isn't risky. But I have faith in [player name], here. The first human to kill a vyre, and quite the vyre it was. Vanescula would be unwise to try anything.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Speaking of [player name], about that other matter I mentioned in my message...

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Ah, of course. [player name], after due consideration by Veliaf and I, we would like to promote you to the rank of lieutenant in the Myreque. Congratulations!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You've done well, friend. I realise it doesn't mean much, but we wanted to thank you in some way for all of your work so far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. It's a great honour.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I wish we had time to celebrate, but time is in short supply these days. I imagine Vanescula will be here very soon. Are we ready?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: As ready as we'll ever be. Let's do this.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well Vanescula, here we are. All the Myreque, just like you wanted.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Your move.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I'm impressed Veliaf. I did fear you'd go and do something silly.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Skip the small talk. Why are we here?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Fine. Conversing with you is a chore, anyway.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: The reason I have gathered you all here is because I have an offer for you.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Now I won't deny, you've not done a bad job. Killing Ranis was no small achievement, but you managed it all the same.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Unfortunately for you, you're still no closer to the true prize.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Lowerniel Drakan.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Indeed. That is why I have come to you. Only with my help will you have any hope of killing my brother.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: And what do you gain from this?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Long ago, the land of Morytania was known as Hallowvale and it was ruled over by a race of Saradominists called the icyene.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: But then your brother came along.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Correct. Hallowvale was taken over by my kind, and Lowerniel became ruler of the newly named Morytania.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: And you know what? It was the worst thing that ever happened to us.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: My brother was a great conqueror, make no mistake. But he is a poor ruler. And now, thanks to him, our society is collapsing.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Collapsing how?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I know you've already been investigating Damien Leucurte and his not so secret arrangement with some of Darkmeyer's nobles.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Tell me, have you worked out why this arrangement exists?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We could have a good guess.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Well I can give you better than guesses. Allow me to offer some clarity.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Every day that goes by, the number of humans in Morytania decreases. Yet while this happens, our population continues to grow. Unsurprisingly, we no longer have enough blood to go around.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Yeah, yeah. We've suspected as much for a long time. Why is this relevant?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: It's relevant because while all of this happens, our great leader remains absent. Lowerniel never liked to show his face, but these days it's as if he doesn't even exist. He spends months in his chambers, refusing to see anyone.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: So you wish to take his place. What makes you think we'll help you with that?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: There was once a time where humans and vampyres lived together in a great empire. Under my rule, I would see us return to that way of life.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Morytania would become a land of peace and prosperity, with humans and vampyres living side by side.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Or we could just kill both you and your brother and take back Morytania for humanity!

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Ha! You could try. Even if you were to succeed though, which isn't likely, one of the other vampyre lords or ladies would just take over instead.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: No. If you want to save your people, my way is the only way.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Why do you need us though? Why not just kill your brother yourself?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I could, but even today there are many in Darkmeyer who still blindly believe in Lowerniel. It will be much easier for me to take his place if I am not responsible for his demise.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: You'd have us become your scapegoats.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Is that such a bad thing? Your people would consider you heroes. You'd be the ones to finally kill Lowerniel Drakan.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: How do we know you won't just kill us once this is done?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: You don't. But Castle Drakan is impenetrable. You'll never get inside on your own, and Lowerniel clearly has no intention of coming out. This is your only option.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Oh, it's probably also worth mentioning that I can't afford any loose ends. If you decline, I would expect you to leave Morytania immediately.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: To stay would have dire consequences for your life expectancy.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Say we accept your offer. How do you propose to get us into the castle?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Ah yes, the plan itself. You will pose as my prisoners and I shall escort you to Castle Drakan. Your capture is something that even Lowerniel will be unable to ignore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And once inside, we kill him.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Exactly. A nice and simple plan.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Too simple, if you ask me.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Never happy are you Veliaf?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Of course I'm not happy. You expect us to just hop into bed with you? You are the enemy! You always have been, and you always will be! We will have no part in this.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Come now, don't be so hasty. You should at least think things over. While you do, I would appreciate a private chat with Safalaan.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Me?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Yes, you. Join me by the large mausoleum at the back of the graveyard. There is something we need to discuss.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: What makes you think we're just going to let you walk off with him?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: This is growing boringly repetitive, Veliaf. For the last time, if I wanted any of you dead, I'd have done it by now.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: It's alright Veliaf. I will go.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Hmm. Well [player name] has to go with you. I want him there in case Vanescula here tries anything.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Very well. Safalaan, [player name], if you'll please join me.

sections.title: The secret of Safalaan Hallow, part 1
Speaking to the Myreque before unlocking the door

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Any luck with the lock, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Try to hurry. I don't like these games that Vanescula is playing.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: You need to get this door open. From what I can tell, you'll need to ensure that the total value of each row and column on the lock matches the number at the end.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Now, off you go.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Keep an eye on Safalaan, [player name]. I don't like leaving him alone with that vampyre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Hey [player name]. You need to be over with Safalaan and Vanescula. We'll talk later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. See you soon.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Best we don't talk right now, [player name]. You need to be watching Safalaan and Vanescula and I don't want to distract you.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Shouldn't you be with Safalaan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, I'd better go to him.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Now's not the time for chat, [player name]. You're meant to be watching Safalaan and Vanescula.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: You should be keeping an eye on Safalaan and Vanescula. We will talk later.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Safalaan Hallow. Interesting name, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What of it?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: The name 'Hallow'. Do you know where it comes from?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: It's common in these parts. They say it was the name of the old Saradominist rulers.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Spot on. You know your history. The family of icyene that ruled over Hallowvale were the Hallow family. And so of course, the name of the region is no coincidence.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I'm surprised your brother didn't have it renamed 'Drakanvale'.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Good name, but not really his style I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What's your game, Vanescula? Why do you care about my name?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Do you not find it odd that you share a name with the former royal family?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: No, I don't. Like I say, around here the name is pretty ordinary.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Ah, but you are far from ordinary, aren't you Safalaan? Tell me, how much do you remember of your childhood?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Nothing. I remember nothing of my childhood. You can thank your friend Vanstrom for that. He always wanted me dead, even back before the Myreque were a thing. One day he nearly succeeded.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: But your powers stopped him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know about Safalaan's powers?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I know many things about Safalaan. It's why we are here, in fact. But please, go on.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: It's why we're here? What do you mean? What is all this?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Finish your story. I promise that everything will be made clear.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Fine, but it's starting to seem like you already know everything anyway.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: You're right about my powers. I was able to use them to drive Vanstrom away. It came at a cost though.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I remember nothing of the event, nor of anything that happened in my life before it. The only thing I remembered was my name.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: There, story told. Now, tell me, why are we having this conversation?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: This graveyard is no normal graveyard. It was once the private graveyard of the Hallow family. Every member of the royal family was buried here.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: When she was still in power, the ruler of Hallowvale, Queen Efaritay Hallow, had this mausoleum built. It was constructed to one day house her and her human husband, King Ascertes Hallow.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Inside, I believe you will find the truth that you've spent your whole life searching for.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Stop with these games! If you know something, tell me!

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Would you actually believe me if I did? To you, my words are just that, words. Inside this mausoleum lies something far more reliable, proof.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The door is locked.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Well noted. We'll need to unlock it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I bet I can get it open.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Wonderful. It's an interesting lock. The icyene always were devious. From what I can tell, you'll need to ensure that the total value of each row and column matches the number at the end.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Now, off you go.

sections.title: The secret of Safalaan Hallow, part 2

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's a plaque on the coffin.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What does it say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It reads:

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Here lies Larina Hallow. Beloved daughter of Efaritay and Ascertes Hallow and...'

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My god...

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What does it say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: '...and faithful sister of Safalaan Hallow.'

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I... I don't...

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: No! It can't be true! It can't be!

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I'm sorry Safalaan, but it is true. You are the half- human, half-icyene son of Queen Efaritay Hallow.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: A sister... I had a sister. And my powers?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: The powers of an icyene, yes.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: But... I don't understand...

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I realise it's a lot to take in.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: No. What I mean is, you said that this tomb was built for Efaritay and Ascertes, for my mother and father. But they're not here.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Where are they Vanescula?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Safalaan I...

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: No! No more games! I know why you've done this. You don't care for me. You're just trying to manipulate me!

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Well fine, it worked. If you want my support in killing your brother, you will have it. But you must tell me, where are my parents?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: The war for this land was long and bloody. We were winning, but with heavy losses. Lowerniel didn't mind. He was a hunter and a warrior. In his eyes, there was no death more noble than one in battle.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Many didn't share that view though, and wanted the war to be over. Ranis was one of them. He managed to infiltrate Efaritay's castle. Once inside, he captured your father, Ascertes.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Efaritay surrendered that same day. She walked into Lowerniel's camp, alone, and willingly handed herself in. I guess she had hoped that Lowerniel would free your father in exchange for her.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: But he didn't, did he?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: No. After that, I'm afraid I don't know what happened to your mother. No one ever saw her again. Only Lowerniel himself knows her fate.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: And my father?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Like I said, we suffered heavy losses in the war. We needed a way to quickly restore our numbers. Vampyrification was the answer. Your father was the first of many to go through the process.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: You turned him into a vampyre? You monsters!

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Lowerniel turned him into a vampyre, not I.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: But you were there, by his side. Do you expect me to believe that you're not just like him?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I can't control what you believe, but I am not the enemy here.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What happened then? After you had your way with him, what became of my father?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: He still lives to this day, under a different name of course.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What name? Tell me!

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Vanstrom Klause.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: No! You're lying! You said it yourself earlier, I have no reason to believe you!

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Everything I have told you is the truth. Only you can decide if you want to believe it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Safalaan...

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: My family...

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Alright Vanescula, you win. If you want Lowerniel dead, you will have the support of the Myreque. I ask only one thing.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Which is?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: When the time comes, I will be the one to kill him. He will pay for what he has done to me and my family.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: So be it. You should inform the rest of your group.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: [player name], I need some time to think over what we have discovered here. Please, inform the others for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Now, both of you leave. I need some time alone.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanted to ask you something.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: No. You need to tell your companions about Safalaan's decision. Now, get going.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Myreque prevail, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Myreque prevail, Vertida.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Have you spoken to the others about the plan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm doing that now.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Well I'll let you get back to it then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See you later.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Myreque prevail, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Myreque prevail, Vertida.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: I saw you head into that mausoleum with Safalaan and Vanescula. What's going on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Turns out this graveyard is more than just a quiet meeting place. That mausoleum contained the truth behind Safalaan's heritage.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Truth? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Safalaan is the only living child of Efaritay Hallow, the former queen of Hallowvale.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: By Saradomin!

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Is he okay? That's quite the sudden discovery.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He wishes to be alone. In the meantime, he asked me to speak to everyone.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: He's agreed to work with her, hasn't he?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you know?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: I know what he's like. I've known Safalaan longer than anyone. When Vanstrom attacked him and he lost his memory, I was the one that found him.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Well, if Safalaan wants to work with Vanescula, then he has my support.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. That was easy. I was expecting you to disagree with him.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: I trust Safalaan. He's made some mistakes, sure. But he's a good leader and I know he won't have made this choice lightly. Tell him he has my support all the way on this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, thanks Vertida.

sections.title: Talking to Polmafi again before talking to Radigad

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I was wondering if you'd changed your mind about working with Vanescula?

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: I'm afraid not. Killing Lowerniel Drakan is no victory at all if he just gets replaced with his sister. Especially if it results in our demise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. I'll see what the others think.

sections.title: Talking to Polmafi again after talking to Radigad

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I was wondering if you'd changed your mind about working with Vanescula?

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: I'm afraid not. Killing Lowerniel Drakan is no victory at all if he just gets replaced with his sister. Especially if it results in our demise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well maybe it doesn't have to. Radigad has a plan of his own. He thinks that once her brother is dead, we should kill Vanescula as well.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: An interesting proposal. Do you think we can do it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If we can kill Lowerniel, we can kill her. He's meant to be the most powerful vampyre around.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: A fair point. And I guess if we can't kill Lowerniel, what does it matter at that point. We'll all be dead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A pleasant thought. So, are you still against the plan?

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: I still fear it's a trap. But perhaps our best move now is to spring that trap. You've convinced me. I will support Safalaan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Polmafi again after convincing him

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Hello there. Have you convinced the others to work with Vanescula?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Well keep at it.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Hello there. I hope Vanescula isn't causing trouble.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet she isn't. In fact, Safalaan has decided he wants to work with her.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: He does? Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She showed him the truth about his family. That seemed to convince him.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Oh dear. I fear she is trying to manipulate him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you don't agree with his decision?

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Vanescula does not strike me as someone that fails to consider every detail of a plan. However, you must have spotted the clear holes in this scheme to kill her brother.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Her plan detailed getting us in and killing her brother. But there was no mention of getting us out again. We'll have just killed the ruler of Morytania. To the vampyres, we'll be public enemy number one.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: If you ask me, I reckon that Vanescula will kill us all as soon as her brother is out of the picture.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you don't support the plan?

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: I'm afraid not. Killing Lowerniel Drakan is no victory at all if he just gets replaced with his sister. Especially if it results in our demise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. I'll see what the others think.

sections.title: Talking to Ivan again after convincing him

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Hey [player name]. Any luck with Veliaf?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: He can't say no forever. If everyone else is on board, he'll have to agree.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's hope so. Talk later Ivan.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Hey [player name]. I've been pondering what Vanescula told us about Calsidiu.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: I understand why Veliaf lied at the time. We were on the brink of defeat. Without a bit of hope, we'd have probably never made it to this point.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: But I don't think we need that false hope any more. Now, we have real hope. Ranis Drakan is dead! Ranis Drakan! He was Lowerniel Drakan's own brother!

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: If Ranis can die, so can the rest. Lowerniel's days are numbered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're not angry at Veliaf?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: No. He did what he believed was right. In the end, that's all any of us can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well on that note, how do you feel about Vanescula's plan?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: It's dangerous, but so what? We're the Myreque, danger is what we do. I say we go for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems you're not alone with that sentiment. Safalaan wishes to work with Vanescula.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: You have my support [player name]. It's Veliaf you need to convince.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I'd better talk to him then.

sections.title: Talking to Radigad again after convincing him

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: [player name]. How goes it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working to convince the others to work with Vanescula.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: You should let them know my plan. I bet that will convince some of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good idea. Thanks Radigad.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: [player name]. How goes it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Safalaan has decided he wants to work with Vanescula.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Has he now? A good decision if you ask me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You think so?

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Oh yes. Here's how I see it. Vanescula is right, we can't kill her brother without her help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I feel like there's a 'but' coming on.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: But... once the deed is done, we don't have any further need of her. We kill her as well and Morytania is finally free of that wretched family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You heard what she said. If we kill her another vampyre could just take her place.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Well of course she said that. She needs us to believe that we need her, otherwise her whole plan is done for.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Think about it. Lowerniel Drakan is supposedly the most powerful vampyre in existence. What vampyre would dare cross the humans that killed him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You make a fair point. I take it you're happy to work with Vanescula then?

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Of course! Let's rid Morytania of evil, once and for all!

sections.title: Talking to Kael again after convincing him

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Good to see you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you, Kael.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: I'd better not disturb you. You need to tell the others about the plan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Good to see you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you, Kael. I wanted to talk to you about Vanescula's plan.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Appears risky to me, friend. Vampyres are the enemy and she is a vampyre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We take risks every day though, don't we?

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: We do. But some we take because we have to, others because we choose to.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: We took an unnecessary risk back in Meiyerditch with Serafina. Look how that turned out. Flaygian, Mekritus and Andiess. All dead because of that risk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Were you close to them?

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Flaygian and Mekritus? Not particularly. They were good men, and it was sad to lose them, but we were not close.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Now, Andiess on the other hand, he and I grew up together. Before Safalaan found us, he was all I had. But now he's gone and nothing I do will ever bring him back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you blame Safalaan?

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: No. He could not have known what would happen. But that doesn't mean we should take more unnecessary risks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This could be our only chance to kill Lowerniel Drakan. Surely that makes the risk necessary?

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: I want to believe that, I really do. I just don't know if I'm ready to lose any more friends.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't promise there won't be losses. But what of the losses we've already suffered? If we don't kill Lowerniel Drakan, surely they were all for nothing?

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: I... I guess you're right. Okay then, I'm in. Let's kill him, for Andiess, and for everyone else who's suffered because of him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He'll regret the day he crossed the Myreque.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf again before convincing everyone else

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Veliaf. Have you thought any more about working with Vanescula?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: There is no more thinking to do. The answer is still no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf again after convincing everyone else

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Veliaf. Have you thought any more about working with Vanescula?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: There is no more thinking to do. The answer is still no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, all the others have agreed to the plan. It's just you.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: All of them? But why?
