lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They all have their reasons. They all seem valid reasons to me.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: But they must know that we can't trust Vanescula.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think they know that. We do not necessarily need to trust her to work with her.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Damn it. I don't want to work with her, but I have no intention of abandoning the others to their fate. Fine. Looks like we all get to die together.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Come. I guess we should tell that vampyre that we're in.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met. Tell me, what's Vanescula up to with Safalaan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Turns out Vanescula knows a lot about Safalaan. More than he knows about himself in fact.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That mausoleum contained the truth about Safalaan's family. He is the only surviving child of Hallowvale's queen, Efaritay Hallow.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: No! It can't be!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Are you sure this isn't a trick? It could be a trap set by Vanescula?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Possibly, though I don't think so. It all adds up. What else could explain his strange abilities?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Hmm. I guess it does fit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not the only thing. Vanescula told us that Safalaan's father still lives, but as a vampyre. Vanstrom Klause.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Those monsters! How has Safalaan taken this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As well as can be expected. However, he has decided it's best that we work with Vanescula.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: What? This is a bad idea! She is a vampyre, we can't trust her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you won't work with her?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: No. I'm sorry, but no. She's trying to manipulate Safalaan but I see through her lies. I can't support this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Keep an eye on Safalaan, won't you? I worry about how he's taking things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I shall, fear not.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Good. Now you should head off to that laboratory. No point wasting time.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanted to ask you something.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Be quick then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: You're meant to be searching the Haemalchemy Laboratory for more research. Run along now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you hate your brother?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Why wouldn't I? He's an awful leader. My kind are slowly starving to death thanks to overpopulation, but he doesn't even seem to have noticed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is killing him not a bit much?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Not really. It is the vampyre way. There was once a time when things were far more savage. Back on our homeworld, new clan leaders were selected during a festival known as the Night of the Blood Moon.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: It was a simple concept. A full night of killing. At dawn, the one with the most kills became the new leader. Lowerniel loved it. He won every time of course. Personally, I prefer our new way of life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And yet you're still happy to kill him?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: We are not the beasts we were back then, but some things do not change. Like I say, it is the vampyre way.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Now, did you want something else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's all, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did Ranis capture Ascertes Hallow?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Do you know of Verzik Vitur?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Owner of the Theatre of Blood? Who doesn't know of her?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I'd give her the credit over Ranis. The two of them shared a love for playing with their food rather than eating it. They hatched all kinds of schemes together.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: During the war, they managed to capture a justiciar, one of Efaritay's most elite warriors. They tortured him for days, until he gave them what they needed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which was?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: A secret tunnel, leading directly into Efaritay's castle. The tunnel was here, in this very graveyard. Once they were inside, capturing Ascertes was easy.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: In some ways, I think Lowerniel was a bit disappointed the fighting was over. The pair were still greatly rewarded, however. Verzik ended up getting Efaritay's castle. It was quite the boon for her.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Now, did you want something else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did Lowerniel shut down the Haemalchemy Laboratory?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I can only assume he didn't like what they were doing there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well obviously. Could you be more specific though?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Are you familiar with their areas of research?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ways to overcome blood shortages and ways to improve your immunity to our weapons.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Essentially. The first might seem to benefit Lowerniel, but when you look deeper you can see why he wasn't a fan.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: For our kind to be less reliant on humans would be a fundamental change to our society. History has told us that leaders generally don't survive such a transition.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: As for the second, I think it's obvious why Lowerniel wanted to put a stop to that. Just ask Ranis why that research maybe wasn't overly wise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. I guess that makes sense.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Indeed. Of course, Lowerniel's long absences mean that a lot of research was already complete before the laboratory was shut down. Hence how you were able to use it against Ranis.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which we were only able to do because of you. Why did you send Sarius to us?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: A test. Even back then I knew that Lowerniel needed to go. I wanted to see if a partnership with the Myreque was worth exploring.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Now, did you want something else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, never mind.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Meet me at the laboratory, friend. Let's see what we can discover.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see you there.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: These are dark times friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They are. But maybe there's a light at the end of the tunnel.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Maybe. I guess we'll find out soon enough. Be careful out there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Myreque prevail, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Myreque prevail, Vertida.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: We'll soon be doing battle with one of the most powerful beings in existence. How are you feeling?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm okay. How about you?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Surprisingly calm, to be honest. We may well die in this fight, but if we manage to defeat Drakan, it will be worth it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess it's not a bad way to go.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Exactly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See you later.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Imagine, the death of Lowerniel Drakan. Think what that could do to these dark lands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's crazy to think it might actually happen.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: We've come a long way. Just one final hurdle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then, peace in our time.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Let us hope, let us hope.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Hey [player name]. Looks like it's really happening. We're finally going to show Drakan what happens when you cross the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looks like it. How are you feeling?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Oh I'm great. I know we can do this, especially with you on our side.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hope you're right to have faith in me.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Don't worry, I know you won't let us down.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Good to see you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you, Kael.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Do you think we're ready for this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The fight with Drakan? Yes, I think so. We killed Ranis after all.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Lowerniel's a much bigger fish though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He is. But it's not just me this time. This time there's all of us. And Vanescula too, assuming she doesn't betray us.

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: Let us hope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well Vanescula, it seems you have managed to convince us. Well done.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Thank you Veliaf. Now, shall we crack on?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What must we do?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: If you want any hope of defeating my brother, you'll need the proper weaponry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We have weaponry. We have the Ivandis Flail.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Indeed you do. Not a bad weapon by any means, but not ideal for taking on Lowerniel. He'll snap it in two before you even have a chance to use it.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: It worked well enough on Ranis.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Lowerniel is far more powerful than Ranis was. If you want to defeat him, you should take my advice.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Fine. What are you proposing?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: If I'm not mistaken, which I'm generally not, you made that weapon using research from the Haemalchemy Laboratory.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: How did you know that?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: [player name], who was it that helped you find that laboratory?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sarius Guile, your servant. Hang on... Did you send her?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Of course I did. Do you think she would have dared interact with you without my consent? Come now, surely you're smarter than that?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Anyway, I don't know much of the laboratory myself. It was Ranis that had it set up. Lowerniel wasn't very happy when he found out.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: That said, I do suspect it might have more secrets for us to discover.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you want us to go back there?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Yes. If you are able to find further research notes, I suspect we'll find a way to upgrade that weapon of yours.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'd better be off to the laboratory then.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I would like to join you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Are you sure, Safalaan?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Yes. Better I be out there doing something than be stuck here brooding.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: [player name], do you remember how to reach the laboratory?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's in sector six of Meiyerditch, just south east of the daeyalt mine.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Very well. I will meet you there.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Good luck to you both.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan before being ready

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Shall we head in?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I need to prepare first.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Well just let me know when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Good to see you, [player name]. Glad you made it here safely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you. How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: To be honest, I'm not sure if I've even properly taken it in yet. It's all so much. My mother, my sister... and of course, my father.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: It feels strange. I feel like I should mourn, but how do I mourn a family I can't even remember?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: And Vanstrom... My greatest enemy may well be my only remaining family. I wonder, is there anything left of Ascertes now? Is part of him still in there, buried away?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Probably best not thinking about, I suppose. My job is to lead the Myreque. I can't let anything else distract me from that, not even my family.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: With all that's happened, I fear some of them may already be beginning to doubt me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have their support Safalaan, I know you do.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I hope so. I let them down with that Serafina business, and people died. I can't let them down again.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Anyway, let us crack on. It seems that we should be able to access more of the laboratory through the doors to the south.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: They were damaged and couldn't be opened, but I think I've managed to fix them up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't know you had a knack for Construction?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Ah, one of my hidden talents. Of course, not much room for that sort of thing around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a shame. I'm sure you'd make a great builder.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Ha! It's strange to think how differently our lives could have been, is it not? Alas, here we are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry. Maybe now isn't the best time for these kinds of conversations.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: You have a good sense of humour my friend, even in these dark times. Do not be ashamed of that. When things are darkest, a bit of light is very welcome.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess you're right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shall we get going then?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Indeed. Let's head in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Well just let me know when you're ready to go in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright there old-timer?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I'm starting to regret praising your humour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to be doing?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: You need to try and find some more haemalchemy research. There must be something of note around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you alright Safalaan?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I... I'm fine. What happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We were attacked by some bloodvelds. You used your powers to kill one.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Blasted powers! What good are powers when I have no control over them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't be so harsh on yourself. Your powers have got us out of a couple of sticky situations now.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Yes, but what happens if I totally lose control? Will I lose my memory like that time with Vanstrom? Or will I do something even worse?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well now that we know the source of your powers, maybe we can find a way to help you control them.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Maybe... Let us hope we can do so before it's too late.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Bah, look at me, [player name]. I'm meant to be a leader, but I can barely control my own body. I'm like an old man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... you are the son of Queen Efaritay. I guess that makes you thousands of years old.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Eugh, what an awful thought. I guess I should get myself a walking stick.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure we can arrange that.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Let's add it to the list. First though, the research we came here for. I'm afraid I'm still feeling a bit weak, so you'll have to do the heavy lifting on this one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh I see how it is! You find out you're an old-timer and now I have to do all the work!

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Afraid so. My poor back simply can't take it. I've no doubt you'll manage though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I'll have a look round and see what I can find. You stay here, old-timer.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Please stop calling me that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can make no promises.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think we can kill Drakan?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: To be honest, I don't know. They say he's the most powerful being in existence. The truth is though, we know pretty much nothing about him.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I guess the point is that we're in the best position possible. If we can't kill him, I suspect no one can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We seem to be dealing with a lot of uncertainties.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Oh we are. But you play the hand you're dealt, my friend. This is our hand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess. Losing a card game generally doesn't result in death though.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: You should try playing against a werewolf.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of Vanescula?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I don't trust her. She feigned affection back at the graveyard. But I know she's just doing it for her own gain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You still chose to work with her though.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Yes. She is our only hope of defeating her brother. In truth, I'd pretty much made my choice even before she told me about my family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's good to know. I worried you were being too hasty after you discovered the truth.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: You are not wrong to worry, friend. I may well be leading us to certain death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you have our support. For better or worse.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Thank you. I appreciate it, especially from you. As an outsider, you could easily turn and run at any time. The fact that you're still here speaks volumes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could we talk more about your family?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Not now.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I'm sorry. I know you must have so many questions. I have plenty myself. But I need to focus right now and cannot be distracted by such thoughts.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: We will talk later. You have my word.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back soon.

sections.title: Talking to Safalaan after getting the book
Talking to Safalaan after giving the book

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: We should get back to the graveyard. I'll meet you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I'll see you soon.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Any luck?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I found some more haemalchemy research.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Good work. I'll look after it for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Right. We'd better get back to the graveyard. I'll meet you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could we talk more about your family?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Not now.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I'm sorry. I know you must have so many questions. I have plenty myself. But I need to focus right now and cannot be distracted by such thoughts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I'll see you soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to keep hold of it.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Come now, [player name]. We need that research. Please give it to me as soon as you can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still here Ivan?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Yup. Most of the others went to the Meiyerditch hideout. But I wanted to stick around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: This graveyard is one of the last remaining examples of what Hallowvale was like. I bet there's a lot to discover here.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Maybe once Drakan is gone, we can get some proper archaeologists out here. I'm sure they could learn all sorts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I bet they could. You holding up okay?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Oh yes. We're going to win this, I know it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem very confident?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Why wouldn't I be? I have faith in Saradomin. I have faith in Safalaan and Veliaf. I have faith in you. Together, we will beat Drakan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do hope so.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Have faith, friend. Anyway, I'd best let you get on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take care Ivan.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf, Safalaan, or Vanescula

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You should get going friend. That disguise won't sort itself out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to be doing?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Go and buy a vyrewatch disguise from Trader Sven. His house is the one directly south of Old Man Ral's, in sector one of Meiyerditch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And once I have the disguise?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Deliver it to Polmafi at the Meiyerditch hideout, in Old Man Ral's basement.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I shall return later.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanted to ask you something.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Be quick then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: You're meant to be working with Polmafi to create a suitable disguise. Now, off you go.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: ...of your arrogance. What makes you so sure we won't also kill you once your brother is dead?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Oh, you could try. Although I doubt it would end well for you.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Now this is exactly what I mean.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Oh lighten up would you. No sense of humour at all.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I am no fool. I am all too aware of my own weaknesses and how you could exploit them. When we take on Lowerniel, I don't intend to be alone.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I have many supporters amongst the vampyres of Darkmeyer. Your small group against all of us? The fight would be brief.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Hiding behind larger numbers? Not very honourable.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Now you see, I've tried honour before. Decided it wasn't for me. Honour matters not if you're dead. I intend to keep living.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Ah, [player name], you're back. Good to see you made it okay.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Yes, yes. Wonderful to see you. Shall we get on with things?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Go ahead.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Well, the research you recovered has confirmed something I have long suspected.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Which is?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Queen Efaritay was a renowned botanist. She used this to her advantage many times during the war, breeding all kinds of plant life to use against us.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: One of these plants was the Blisterwood tree. The icyene used the sap of this tree to create poisons that were deadly to vampyres.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: However, I've always suspected the tree might have other uses. Clearly the researchers in the Haemalchemy Laboratory thought the same.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what did they find out?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: It seems that the bark of the Blisterwood tree can be used to create weapons that are deadly to my kind.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Well I'm no scientist, oddly enough. But from what I can tell, even when chopped away, the wood of the tree is very much alive. It shifts and grows in unpredictable ways, rendering our immunities useless.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I see where we're going with this. You want us to use this tree to construct new weapons?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Well... not quite. While not perfect, the work you've already done has potential. I see no need to completely discard it.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Instead, I suggest we use wood from the Blisterwood tree to enhance the weapons you already have. When combined with the wood, I imagine that flail of yours will be quite effective.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Perfect for killing your brother. Assuming you don't kill us first.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Again with this? I've already told you, I have no need to kill you. Once Lowerniel is gone, I envisage a new age, where my kind and yours live in harmony. I need you alive for that.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I'll believe it when I see it.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: That's fine by me.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: We're getting sidetracked here. We need some wood from this Blisterwood tree. Where do we find it?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Ah... that's the tricky bit. I know only of one Blisterwood tree. It lies in Efaritay's old Arboretum, in the middle of Darkmeyer.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: It's in the capital? We'll have no hope of getting in there.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I don't see why we'd need to. Vanescula here can get some of this wood for us.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Really now? Do you think I have any intention of touching a tree that can kill vampyres? No. You will need to recover the wood yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Darkmeyer isn't as impenetrable as you might think. I know of a way you can get in. But first, you'll need a disguise.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Now, luckily for us, my kind have some shapeshifting abilities. Many vampyres, such as myself, prefer to take on a human form. That means you should be able to fit in... with the right outfit of course.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Trader Sven sells vyrewatch disguises. We've used them in the past.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Not bad, but we'll want to make some adjustments. We need you looking less like a vyrewatch and more like a noble.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Polmafi knows how to use a needle. He should be able to sort us out.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Good idea. [player name], could you pick up a vyrewatch disguise from Trader Sven? He lives in sector one of Meiyerditch, directly south of Old Man Ral's house.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do. I'm guessing I should take it to Polmafi after that?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: That's right. He and the others have gone back to the Meiyerditch hideout, in Old Man Ral's basement.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Once you have the disguise, we will need to scent it. We will discuss that once you have it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back when I have the disguise.

sections.title: Talking to Polmafi if the player already have a vyrewatch outfit

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to look like a vampyre noble. Safalaan suggested we use a vyrewatch disguise from Trader Sven as a starting point.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: That's a good idea. Do you have a vyrewatch disguise with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I do.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Great. Hand it over and I'll get to work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Here's your finished disguise. Be careful out there.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: There was enough material for some spares. I'll leave them in the chest in the corner in case you need more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll keep it for now.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Well I can't make your new disguise until you give it to me.

sections.title: Talking to Polmafi if the player does not already have a vyrewatch outfit
Talking to Polmafi again without getting a vyrewatch outfit

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Hello there. Do you have a vyrewatch disguise yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: I'll be here once you have.

sections.title: Talking to Polmafi again after getting a vyrewatch outfit

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Hello there. Do you have a vyrewatch disguise yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I do.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Great. Hand it over and I'll get to work.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Good to see you [player name]. How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not too bad. We've managed to recover some more haemalchemy research. We think it can be used to further improve our weaponry.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Well that's good to hear. Anything I can help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, actually. To make the improved weapons, I need to infiltrate the city of Darkmeyer. For that, I need a disguise. Veliaf said you'd be able to help.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: The city of Darkmeyer? Oh my! You'd better be careful.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Anyhow, yes I should be able to help. What kind of disguise do you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to look like a vampyre noble. Safalaan suggested we use a vyrewatch disguise from Trader Sven as a starting point.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: That's a good idea. Do you have a vyrewatch disguise with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Do you mind fetching one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure. I'll see if I can get one from Trader Sven.

sections.title: Kill Two Birds With One Stone
Talking to Vanescula before getting a vyre noble disguise

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Do you have the disguise?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Well we can't go any further then. Return once you have it.

sections.title: Talking to Vanescula after getting a vyre noble disguise

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Do you have the disguise?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it here.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Wonderful. Now, as mentioned earlier, we will need to scent this disguise. My kind have a keen sense of smell and will quickly notice an imposter if they don't smell right.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What do we need to scent it with?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Blood of course. The blood of a vyre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I kill a vyrewatch and use their blood to scent the disguise?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Oh... no, no, no. A vyrewatch will not do. We want you to blend in with the nobles. That means we need the blood of a noble.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: There's a difference?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Of course. The lords and ladies of Darkmeyer won't go interacting with any old rabble.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Of course. Go on then, give us some of your blood.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Oh dear Veliaf, you misunderstand. The smell of my blood will be far too recognisable to work. We need someone a bit more obscure.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Ah yes, how silly of me to not realise. You clearly have a vampyre in mind. Spit it out.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I think we use this as an opportunity to deal with two issues in one. Damien Leucurte has become a problem that needs to be resolved. He is also a noble, albeit a lesser one.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: So we kill Damien and use his blood. Sounds good to me.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Indeed. Let's get it done. [player name], meet me outside the church in Slepe. We're going to pay Damien a little visit.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: If you want, you can use the boat to quickly get to Slepe. Should be faster than walking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good idea. Veliaf, I'll see you there.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Meet me outside the church in Slepe. We're going to pay Damien a little visit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see you there.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: You should meet up with Veliaf in Slepe. We'll talk later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. Until then.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanted to ask you something.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Be quick then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: You need to kill Damien Leucurte and use his blood to scent your disguise. Run along now.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Are you ready to deal with Damien?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met friend. Didn't think we'd be back here so soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know. Not a bad thing though. Damien needs to be dealt with.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I suppose you're right. Now, I've done some scouting. It seems Damien is hiding in the manor. Interestingly, he doesn't seem to have increased his security since Kroy's death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He may well have tried, but I bet we scared off a lot of his supporters.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: They don't want to mess with you my friend. I suspect you're becoming quite notorious amongst the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think we should be able to access the manor easily?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Looks like it. I think we can get in through the dungeon. Let me know when you're ready and we'll head on in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's do this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh wait! I forgot the disguise!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well you'd better go and get it. I'll be here when you are ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think Safalaan is doing okay?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You tell me. You've spent more time with him since his discovery.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He seems to be okay. At least, as okay as anyone could be in that situation. He seems to still be focused, and he's not lost his sense of humour.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Maintaining a good sense of humour is common coping mechanism when dealing with shock. I hope he's not just putting on a facade. If he's struggling, he should seek our support.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not wrong. But that's for him to decide. He knows we are there if he needs us.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: It's good that he has people like you. I may have known him a long time, but I can't pretend to be any good at comforting people in such situations. I never manage to say the right thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you think I do?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You can't be any worse than me. I'd take that as a victory.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think things will improve for him?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I don't know. He may well soon come face to face with Lowerniel Drakan, the cause of all his pain. I hope he is ready for that.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: And then there's Vanstrom, of course. Their paths are likely to cross again before all this is over. And when that happens, who knows what the outcome will be.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Anyway, are you ready to deal with Damien?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you feeling about working with Vanescula?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well, she hasn't killed us yet, which I consider a win. It seems she spoke the truth about wanting her brother dead.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Of course, I still fully expect her to betray us once he's gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you're still willing to go ahead with this?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I wouldn't say I'm 'willing' as such, but what other choice do I have? The others want to do it and I'll be damned if I'm just going to leave them to their fates.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: That doesn't mean I haven't been considering contingencies, of course. I've asked Ivan to prepare us a teleport spell in case we need to make a hasty exit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seems sensible to me. Let's hope it doesn't come to that.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Indeed. But enough about Vanescula. Are you ready to deal with Damien?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: So here you are. I knew it was only a matter of time before you lot showed up.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: We'd hate to disappoint. It's over Damien.

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: Listen to your arrogance! Is this what we've become? I remember when we commanded respect. Your kind would cower in fear at the sight of us!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Once, yes. But now, we are not the ones cowering.

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: You think I'm afraid? I am a lord of Darkmeyer. I am fear!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A lord of Darkmeyer? Isn't a lord meant to have followers? Looks like yours have run away.

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: Cowards! That's what they are. I was their saviour! When our great leaders abandoned us, I was the one that kept them fed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And now you're nothing.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Face it Damien, you're out of time. There's nowhere to run.

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: That is true for all of us!

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: Time to see what you're really made of!

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: Crombwick! You will support me!

lines.speaker: Lord Crombwick
lines.text: What? But I can't...

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: You can and you will!

lines.speaker: Damien Leucurte
lines.text: Come on then! This ends now!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: No need for us to stick around this place any more. I will meet you back at the graveyard.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Got what you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looks like it.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Good. I don't want to stick around this place any longer than needed. I will meet you back at the graveyard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think this means for Slepe?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: The death of Damien can't do any harm. That said, don't expect this to suddenly fix everything around here. The Sleeper Plague isn't the only illness in this town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean their religion?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I do. The people here chose to follow Zamorak. A choice like that has consequences. Bad ones.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You think they are being punished?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Not exactly. I don't believe in fate myself. I'm sure someone like Drezel would say otherwise, though. I just think that if you play with fire, you're going to end up getting burnt.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do you think the Sleeper Plague will go away now?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well there shouldn't be any new cases now that Damien and Kroy are gone. That won't help those already infected though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe there's a cure.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Maybe. I suspect this town holds a lot of secrets... but now is not the time to explore them. We have other business to attend to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see you at the graveyard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think Safalaan is doing okay?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You tell me. You've spent more time with him since his discovery.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He seems to be okay. At least, as okay as anyone could be in that situation. He seems to still be focused, and he's not lost his sense of humour.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Maintaining a good sense of humour is common coping mechanism when dealing with shock. I hope he's not just putting on a facade. If he's struggling, he should seek our support.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not wrong. But that's for him to decide. He knows we are there if he needs us.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: It's good that he has people like you. I may have known him a long time, but I can't pretend to be any good at comforting people in such situations. I never manage to say the right thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you think I do?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You can't be any worse than me. I'd take that as a victory.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think things will improve for him?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I don't know. He may well soon come face to face with Lowerniel Drakan, the cause of all his pain. I hope he is ready for that.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: And then there's Vanstrom, of course. Their paths are likely to cross again before all this is over. And when that happens, who knows what the outcome will be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you feeling about working with Vanescula?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well, she hasn't killed us yet, which I consider a win. It seems she spoke the truth about wanting her brother dead.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Of course, I still fully expect her to betray us once he's gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you're still willing to go ahead with this?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I wouldn't say I'm 'willing' as such, but what other choice do I have? The others want to do it and I'll be damned if I'm just going to leave them to their fates.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: That doesn't mean I haven't been considering contingencies, of course. I've asked Ivan to prepare us a teleport spell in case we need to make a hasty exit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seems sensible to me. Let's hope it doesn't come to that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see you there

sections.title: Talking to Vanescula without the scented disguise

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Do you have the disguise?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I sent some of Damien's blood to Polmafi. Speak to him if you need a replacement disguise.

sections.title: Talking to Vanescula with the scented disguise

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You should get going friend. Darkmeyer awaits.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Good luck. Myreque prevail.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Be careful in Darkmeyer, friend. I can't even imagine how dangerous that city is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I shall be.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanted to ask you something.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Be quick then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: You need to infiltrate Darkmeyer and recover eight logs from the Blisterwood tree. Now, off you go.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Well well, the vampyre slayer returns. Two of my brothers, now Damien as well. Quite the body count you're amassing.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Two of your brothers?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Did you not know? [player name] here isn't just responsible for killing Ranis. He also killed our younger brother, Draynor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Count Draynor, he called himself. He wasn't much of a challenge compared to Ranis and Damien, truth be told.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Ah yes, the great 'Count' Draynor. He loved his stupid titles. He was weak even before he got trapped on the wrong side of the Salve. A coward as well.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: There's a reason Lowerniel had him banished. I'm just surprised he lasted as long as he did.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Now, I assume you have a finished disguise?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Wonderful. Now we're getting somewhere. With that disguise, you should be ready to enter Darkmeyer.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: But how does he get into the city? The place is a fortress.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Almost, but not quite. I know of a small weakness in the southern wall. A crack, in sector six of Meiyerditch, just north of the daeyalt mine.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: It should be big enough for you to squeeze through, as long as you haven't recently eaten an excess of cakes.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Seems suspiciously convenient. Why hasn't the crack been filled in?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: My kind are struggling to feed themselves. They've currently got bigger worries than cracks in walls.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Besides, up until now, why would any human even consider entering Darkmeyer? Perhaps you hadn't noticed, my kind is not known for treating humans well.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Although you expect us to live in harmony once this is done. How are we meant to achieve that when each side hates the other?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I never said it would be easy. I don't expect us to kill my brother and then have a big party. It will be work, but given time, that work will pay off.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: We'll see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So that aside, I can use this crack to enter Darkmeyer? What then?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: You'll need to get to Efaritay's Arboretum in the middle of the city. The city architects gave it facelift to help it blend in, but it's still impossible to miss.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Once inside, you'll need to chop some logs from the Blisterwood tree. We'll need enough to arm everyone, so eight logs in total.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Now, whatever happens, you must not let your true identity be exposed. If a member of the Myreque was found in Darkmeyer, the entire city would be locked down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any tips for blending in?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Well keep your disguise on for a start. You'll want a suitable name as well. Your current one isn't very fitting. If anyone asks, you're called [vampyre name].

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: [vampyre name]. It suits you, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [vampyre name], right. Anything else I should know?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I expect you to see many things you consider undesirable while in Darkmeyer, especially where your kind are concerned.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: While you may be tempted to get involved, don't. Stay in character no matter what.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back once I have the logs. Wish me luck.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Good luck [player name]. Myreque prevail.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Myreque prevail [player name].

sections.title: Talking to Mordan before talking to Desmodus

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there.

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: I'm not interested in making small talk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Suit yourself.

sections.title: Talking to Desmodus Lasiurus before attempting to enter the Arboretum

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To go in the Arboretum.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Well you can't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Because nobody can go in the Arboretum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That can't be true. Someone must be allowed in?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [vampyre name]. I'm new here.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Clearly. Well [vampyre name], you're wrong. No one can go in the Arboretum, by order of the Drakan family themselves.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Why do you want to go in, anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you actually care?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: No, but I have a job to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will it take for you to let me in? There must be something you want. Do you want money?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: I have no need for your money.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What then?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Eugh, fine! There's something I need doing. Get it done for me and I'll grant you access.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! What do you need?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: There's a juvinate who was meant to deliver something to me. He hasn't shown up. Find him and tell him that Desmodus Lasiurus is getting impatient.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure thing! What's he meant to be delivering?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Some slaves. You heard of Gadderanks? He was some human that Vanstrom used to help collect blood tithes.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Well, he went and got himself killed by the Myreque. Vanstrom owed me a favour so he agreed to let me have his wife and child.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... I see.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: You got a problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Good. Mordan Nikazsi is the juvinate you're looking for. He'll be somewhere in the lowest tier of the city. Now, get lost!

sections.title: Talking to Desmodus again before finding Mordan

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Did you find Mordan Nikazsi?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Why are you here then? He'll be somewhere in the lowest tier of the city. Go find him!

sections.title: Talking to Desmodus after finding Mordan, but before getting the slaves back

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Did you find Mordan Nikazsi?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He sold your slaves to Alexei Jovkai.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: That filthy wretch! I'll have him killed for this! Go find my slaves and bring them to me!

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Trying to enter the Arboretum.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Well you can't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Because nobody can go in the Arboretum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That can't be true. Someone must be allowed in?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [vampyre name]. I'm new here.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Clearly. Well [vampyre name], you're wrong. No one can go in the Arboretum, by order of the Drakan family themselves.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Why do you want to go in, anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you actually care?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: No, but I have a job to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will it take for you to let me in? There must be something you want. Do you want money?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: I have no need for your money.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What then?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Eugh, fine! There's something I need doing. Get it done for me and I'll grant you access.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! What do you need?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: There's a juvinate who was meant to deliver something to me. He hasn't shown up. Find him and tell him that Desmodus Lasiurus is getting impatient.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure thing! What's he meant to be delivering?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Some slaves. You heard of Gadderanks? He was some human that Vanstrom used to help collect blood tithes.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Well, he went and got himself killed by the Myreque. Vanstrom owed me a favour so he agreed to let me have his wife and child.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... I see.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: You got a problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Good. Mordan Nikazsi is the juvinate you're looking for. He'll be somewhere in the lowest tier of the city. Now, get lost!

sections.title: Talking to Mordan again before finding Desmodus' slaves

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: Why are you back again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Remind me, where can I find Desmodus' slaves?

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: They're not his slaves. They belong to Alexei Jovkai now. He'll be keeping them in his prison north of the Arboretum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like I need to visit this prison then.

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: Fine.

sections.title: Talking to Mordan again after finding Desmodus' slaves

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: Go away then.

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you Mordan Nikazsi?

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: Yes, what of it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Desmodus Lasiurus sent me.

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: Oh great, just what I need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He wants his slaves. When are you planning to deliver them?

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: I'm not. He shan't be getting his slaves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err... Why not?

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: Because I sold them to someone else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Why?

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: Why? Because I'm starving that's why! You lords and ladies probably haven't noticed yet, but you will soon enough. It's only a matter of time before you all run out of blood as well!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you sold them for blood? Did you not think Desmodus would notice?

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: If I didn't do something I'd have been dead anyway. Better a quick death at Desmodus' hands than a slow one due to starvation!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look I don't care what happens between you and Desmodus, I just need to recover those slaves. Who did you sell them to?

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: Don't waste your time, you won't get them back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: Because I sold them to Lord Alexei Jovkai, one of the most powerful vampyres in the city. He looks at you the same way you look at me. He'll never let you have them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll see. Where can I find him?

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: He lives in the upper part of the city, but the slaves won't be there. He has a prison north of the Arboretum. That's where you'll find them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like I need to visit this prison then.

lines.speaker: Mordan Nikazsi
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: Leave me alone. I have a job to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Is your name Gadderanks?

lines.speaker: Maria Gadderanks
lines.text: Yes... I'm Maria Gadderanks. But... You're not one of Lord Jovkai's lot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No I'm not. Are you doing okay?

lines.speaker: Maria Gadderanks
lines.text: I... I think so. We only arrived here a short while ago, but they've already given us food and some new clothes for Ron.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... What?

lines.speaker: Prisoner
lines.text: You must be new around here. Lord Jovkai isn't like the rest of your lot. He keeps us well looked after.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's taking care of you?

lines.speaker: Prisoner
lines.text: Oh yes. A nice bed, good food and clean clothes. All he asks in return is a bit of blood from time to time. Lovely guy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't...

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: Hey you! What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was just talking to the... prisoners?

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: Well clear off! These are Lord Jovkai's personal tithes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Lord Alexei Jovkai
lines.text: What's all this noise? What's going on in here?

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: It's this vyre, my lord. They're here to cause trouble.

lines.speaker: Lord Alexei Jovkai
lines.text: You! Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm [vampyre name]. And I'm not here to cause any trouble, thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Lord Alexei Jovkai
lines.text: I will be the judge of that, [vampyre name]. Now, why are you here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: These prisoners of yours, they were promised to Desmodus Lasiurus.

lines.speaker: Lord Alexei Jovkai
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's a guard at the Arboretum.

lines.speaker: Lord Alexei Jovkai
lines.text: Ah, so he's a nobody. Well, these prisoners are mine, I paid for them. You'll have to tell him to find some different ones.

lines.speaker: Maria Gadderanks
lines.text: Yes... It seems nice here. We'd like to stay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Jovkai! What's going on here?

lines.speaker: Lord Alexei Jovkai
lines.text: Nothing for you to concern yourself with Vanstrom. I have this under control.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: It doesn't seem that way.

lines.speaker: Lord Alexei Jovkai
lines.text: Tell me, is it now protocol for the head of the vyrewatch to come and investigate every disturbance?

lines.speaker: Lord Alexei Jovkai
lines.text: Surely you have bigger problems, like the blood shortages amongst the commoners?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: As it so happens I was on my way to the lower city to look into that very issue. However, on my way past, I heard shouting.

lines.speaker: Lord Alexei Jovkai
lines.text: And you decided to investigate? How convenient. Tell me, is Lord Drakan really so interested in my business that he's now having you spy on me?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: You're paranoid, Jovkai. Lord Drakan doesn't care about you.

lines.speaker: Lord Alexei Jovkai
lines.text: No. Many are starting to wonder if he cares about any of us.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Careful there, Jovkai. Now tell me, what's happening here?

lines.speaker: Lord Alexei Jovkai
lines.text: This vyre was trying to steal some of my prisoners. I was having them removed.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Hang on... I know those humans. That's the Gadderanks family. They don't belong to you.

lines.speaker: Lord Alexei Jovkai
lines.text: What do you mean? I paid for them!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: That doesn't make them yours. These prisoners belong to Desmodus Lasiurus. You will have them returned to him at once.

lines.speaker: Lord Alexei Jovkai
lines.text: Or what? You don't get to order me around!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: You may be a lord, but you still answer to the Drakan family. That means you also answer to me.

lines.speaker: Lord Alexei Jovkai
lines.text: And what do you think Lasiurus will do with them if I give them to him? They're safe here. They'll be looked after.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I do not care what Lasiurus does with them any more than I care about you and your weird tendencies.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Now, we're done here. See that the prisoners are returned, or I'll have this place shut down.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: You there, vyre!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ye... Yes?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: You will return to Lasiurus and tell him that his prisoners are on their way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Wait... Your voice, vyre. It sounds... familiar.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: What is your name?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [vampyre name]. I'm new here.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Clearly. Which makes me wonder why I'd know your voice.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Turn and face me. I want to get a good look at you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Turn, and face me. I will not ask again.

lines.speaker: Sarius Guile
lines.text: Vanstrom!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Eugh, what now Guile?

lines.speaker: Sarius Guile
lines.text: Forgive me for the interruption, but Lady Vanescula has urgent need of you.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Fine. My business here is done, regardless.

lines.speaker: Maria Gadderanks
lines.text: I thought we might do okay here but I guess we have another new owner now.

lines.speaker: Vyrewatch
lines.text: Why are you still here? Go tell Lasiurus that his slaves will be with him soon.

sections.title: Talking to Desmodus after retrieving the slaves

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I go in the Arboretum?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Yes. Now leave me alone.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Have you sorted my slaves out?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's all dealt with. They'll be with you soon.

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Good. Maybe you're not as useless as you look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can go in the Arboretum now, then?

lines.speaker: Desmodus Lasiurus
lines.text: Yes. Just don't tell anyone I let you in.

lines.speaker: Banker
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to access my bank account.

lines.speaker: Banker
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: Banker
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Banker
lines.text: Newcomers aren't allowed to use the bank. Come back once you've earned our trust.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the Hallowed Sepulchre or Daeyalt essence mines

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have time to poke around down there. I have work to do.

sections.title: Attempting to take a severed leg from a corpse cart

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now's not the time for searching through corpses. I have work to do.

sections.title: Pouring a bucket of water on the blisterwood tree

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you selling?

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: To you? Nothing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: You're new here. I don't sell to new blood. Make a name for yourself and maybe I'll reconsider.

sections.title: Talking to Vanescula without eight blisterwood logs

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: You're back. Do you have the logs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Well run along then. We need eight logs from the blisterwood tree.

sections.title: Talking to Vanescula with eight blisterwood logs

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Deliver those blisterwood logs to Vertida in the Meiyerditch hideout. He'll show you how to make the new weapons out of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanted to ask you something.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Be quick then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: You're meant to be working with Vertida to make the blisterwood weaponry. Run along now.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: You're back. Do you have the logs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have them here.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Good work [player name]. Did it all go okay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was a bit close at times. But I managed it.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: A bit close? You nearly blew everything with your little run in with Vanstrom. It was a good job I had Sarius keep an eye on you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Spying on me?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I just wasn't convinced you'd be able to do the job. It seems I was right to be cautious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had it under control. By the way, where is Safalaan?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: He said he needed a walk. I told him not to, but he insisted he'd be fine.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: You worry too much. Safalaan is a big boy.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Now, [player name]. Time to get these weapons made.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I've given your friend Vertida the required information. He will show you what to do.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: He's at the Meiyerditch hideout. Make sure to take the logs with you. The others need them for their own weapons.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right then. I'll be back once the weapons are made.

sections.title: Talking to Vertida without eight blisterwood logs

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Myreque prevail, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Myreque prevail, Vertida.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: I hear you're meant to be bringing us some logs. Do you have them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: I'll be here once you have them. We need eight in total.

sections.title: Talking to Vertida with eight blisterwood logs

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do again?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: First you'll want to take a blessed silver sickle and use a chisel to embed a ruby in it. There's some blessed sickles in a crate here but you'll need to get your own ruby.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Once that's done, you'll need to enchant the ruby sickle using a ruby enchant spell.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Finally, you'll need to use a knife to combine your sickle with the blisterwood logs. That will give you a blisterwood sickle that you can attach to your flail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great. I'll get going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good, thanks.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Myreque prevail, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Myreque prevail, Vertida.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: I hear you're meant to be bringing us some logs. Do you have them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have them here.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Great. Could I have seven of them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Right, we're going to be using these logs to make blisterwood sickles. That's what most of us will be using in the battle.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: However, I think you'll be able to swap out the sickle on your flail for a blisterwood one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A blisterwood flail. I like the sound of that. So how do we make these sickles?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: It should be very similar to how you made your emerald sickle. First, you'll want a blessed silver sickle. We have plenty in the crate over there.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: You'll want to take your blessed sickle and use a chisel to embed a ruby in it. You'll need to get your own ruby I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Once that's done, you'll need to enchant the ruby sickle using a ruby enchant spell.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Finally, you'll need to use a knife to combine your sickle with the blisterwood logs. That will give you a blisterwood sickle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then I can attach that to my flail?

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Exactly. If you need any help, just let me know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get started.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll keep them for now.

lines.speaker: Vertida Sefalatis
lines.text: Well let me know when you're ready to hand them over.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf without a blisterwood flail

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met, [player name]. Do you have your weapon?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Well off you go then. You'll need it before we go any further.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf with a blisterwood flail

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You need to try and find Safalaan. He went for a walk just north of the graveyard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back once I've found him.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanted to ask you something.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Be quick then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: You're meant to be finding Safalaan. Off you go.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met, [player name]. I'm guessing you have your weapon?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. I'm prepared.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: And you took the logs to the others?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did indeed.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Then we are ready.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Safalaan isn't back yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where he went?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: There's a path just north of the graveyard. He went for a walk in that direction.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see if I can find him.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Don't take too long.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Safalaan.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: So this is it. All these years of fighting. All these years of running. It all ends today. One way or another.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: One way or another.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: You seem troubled?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess that makes two of us.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Ha! Well, I asked first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you remember Gadderanks?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: The human that worked with Vanstrom? Didn't you and Veliaf kill him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We did. Before he died though, he helped us. In return, he asked us to help his family.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found them, in Darkmeyer. They were in a prison, albeit a nice one. The vampyre that owned it looked after his tithes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're not there anymore though, thanks to me. Another vampyre has them now. One that I don't imagine will show any kindness to them.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I assume you had no choice?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. It was the only way for me to get into the Arboretum. That doesn't change what I did, though. I broke my promise to a dying man.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: A man that worked for the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because he had no other choice. Vanstrom would have killed his family otherwise.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: We make the hard choices to spare others from having to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. But this time we weren't the ones who paid for it.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: When Drakan is gone, and Morytania is free, it will all be worth it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If Vanescula keeps her word.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's me. Your turn.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: It's possible we will encounter Vanstrom today.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: And I wonder... is there anything left of the man he once was?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Does the process of vampyrification destroy everything that makes us human? Or does it just hide it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Maybe we'll never know. And yet...

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Our whole order was built upon hope. A hope so small, it might as well have not even existed. But that never stopped us.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: I still cling on to some hope for him. Is that wrong?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's rarely wrong to hope.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Well... We could stand here forever, watching the world go by. It's just delaying the inevitable.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Time to end it.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Oh... One last thing, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Thank you, for everything. It's been a pleasure.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Come. Let us return to the others.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Well it looks like everything is ready.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Yes. Everyone has one of the new blisterwood sickles. They are all prepared.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Good. Here's what's going to happen.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: From here, I will teleport our group to the Darkmeyer Arboretum. There, we will join with some vyres loyal to me.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: We will take your group to Castle Drakan as our prisoners. Once inside, my vyres will ensure no one enters while we deal with my brother.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: And then?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Once Lowerniel is dead, I will teleport you all back here again. I will then announce his death to my kind and take his place as the ruler of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: From there, we will start to build our new kingdom, together.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: We'll see.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Yes we will.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Make sure you're ready for this, [player name]. Once we start, there's no going back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I understand.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Good, good. Once you are ready, let me know and we will begin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: This is it. This is the day we've spent so long fighting for. Myreque prevail.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Myreque prevail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Myreque prevail.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need more time to prepare.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Well don't take too long. Return once you are ready.

sections.title: Talking to Vanescula after Safalaan returns

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Are you ready to deal with my brother?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: So this is it. End of the line. Good luck [player name]. I'll see you on the other side.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good luck Ivan. Myreque prevail.

sections.title: Face-to-face with the Lord of Vampyrium

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Vanescula? What's going on here?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Vanstrom. I am here with a gift for my brother.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
