lines.text: But... that's the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Yes. All the Myreque.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I don't understand. How?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Your efforts were valiant, Vanstrom. But alas, they were unsuccessful. I decided to take matters into my own hands, using a new tactic.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I negotiated with them. Convinced them that I was on their side. After that, bringing them in was trivial.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I... I'm sorry my lady, but this just seems too good to be true.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Well it is true. Now, if you'll excuse me, I'd best be getting inside.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: I rememeber when I was young. Our father would take me out into the woods, hunting.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: That was where I truly discovered what it meant to be alive.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: The constant fear. That knowledge that if you fail, your very life may well be over.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: But then at the same time, the thrill. The thrill of the chase. Of the conquest. Nothing can match it.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: One day, he decided to bring my sister along. He felt it was time for her to develop her own taste for the hunt.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: She was a natural. A raw talent I had never seen before, and rarely since. I felt a unique pride that day.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: That wasn't all, of course. There were so many things she excelled at. So many talents.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: But lying, Vanescula?

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: Lying was never one of them.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Brother. I don't...

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: Tell me, Vanescula. What is the cause of this latest betrayal? How is it I have displeased you this time?

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: I don't know what...

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: Vanescula, please. This veil of lies is thinner than the humans you feast upon.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: Now, the truth.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: You have been gone too long, brother. While you spend your days hidden away, lamenting about that stupid rock, our kind suffer.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Out there, in your city, they're starving. There's not enough blood to go around and their great leader is unwilling to provide a solution.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: Something has to change, or we're doomed.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: Vanescula, my dear sister. You disappoint me.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: You and your kind, you have spent your years engrossing yourself in culture, in aristocracy. Pretending to be something you're not.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: And in that time, you have forgotten something.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: Allow me to remind you.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: I am Lowerniel Vergidiyad Drakan, ruler of Morytania and the lord of Vampyrium.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: And when you play your games of politics, you do not do so because you're smarter than me.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: When you weave your words and make your schemes, you are not getting the better of me.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: No. You do these things only because I allow it. Because your plots are so insignificant to me, that I do not even care to pay attention to them.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: But now Vanescula, now you have my attention. And now, you will be reminded what it means to cross the lord of Vampyrium.

lines.speaker: Vanescula Drakan
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Let's get him!

lines.speaker: Kael Forshaw
lines.text: For Andiess!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: No! Wait!

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Kael! Vertida!

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: Vanstrom.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: My... my lord?

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: Are you still loyal?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Yes, my lord.

lines.speaker: Lowerniel Drakan
lines.text: Good. Deal with the rest of this. My sister and I have much to discuss.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I've waited a long time for this day. The day that the Myreque finally die.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: There'll be no hellhounds or abominations this time though. This time...

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: ...I'll deal with you myself.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Wait!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Safalaan don't!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Well, well. Safalaan Hallow.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: How long I have wished to end your pitiful life...

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Stop! Just stop for a moment and think. This isn't who you really are!

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: You had a life before all of this. A life as Ascertes Hallow, my father.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Please! Think back to those days. Think back to who you used to be.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: You talk too much.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Shall we begin?

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf after leaving the fight

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: We need to get back to Darkmeyer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need more time to prepare.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well be quick about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I've lost my flail.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Luckily for you, I had another made. Although I'll have to charge you for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: 40,000 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have that much.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I'll be here when you do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good to me.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Here you go. Now, are you ready?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a bit too much for me. I guess I'll make myself another one instead.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf after dying during the fight
Right click collect while Veliaf does not have any items

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I don't have anything for you friend. If I did have any of your items, but you died before collecting them, they'll now be lost.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I've managed to retrieve some of your things. I imagine you'll want them back.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Blood will be my strength!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: My pets will feast on your corpse!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Stare into darkness!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Ha! Did you think you could beat me that easily?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: See if you can survive this!

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Enough! Stop this!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Stop? And why would I want to do that?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: Father, please. You must remember something? Anything!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Remember? I remember everything!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I remember how weak Ascertes was! The pathetic love he had for his wife, for his children!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I remember how good it felt! To be uplifted! To be made something more!

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: And I remember the joy when I took your sister's skull and crushed it with my bare hands!

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: No! No! No!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Safalaan!

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Veliaf...

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: [player name]! Safalaan! We need to go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Safalaan?

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What... What have I done?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Ivan, teleport him out of there!

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: I can't! He's too far away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Safalaan please.

lines.speaker: Safalaan Hallow
lines.text: What have I done?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Damn you Safalaan! Damn you!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: [player name], get over here! We need to go. Now!

sections.title: Recovering a lost tome of experience from Ivan

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: [player name]. I have a tome here that I think belongs to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, Ivan.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: He... He killed them. All of them.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Veliaf, what do we do?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Veliaf?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: What do we do? There's nothing we can do. Don't you see, Ivan? It's over. They've won.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: But we can't give up. We're the Myreque!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: The Myreque are dead.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Veliaf?

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: He's gone. They've all gone.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: I'm sorry, [player name]. I need some time alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure? I could take you back to Paterdomus.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: No. I'm staying here.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Take this. It's not much, but it's all I can offer.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Now, please. I'd like to be alone.
